title: Conjecture 4 (Dvořák and Mnich)
statement_text: There exists a constant $c < 3$ such that every planar graph of girth at least five has fractional chromatic number at most $c$.
context_text: Jones found for each $n \equiv 2 \pmod{3}$ an $n$-vertex triangle-free planar graph with fractional chromatic number exactly $3 - \frac{3}{n+1}$; these extremal examples all have many 4-cycles, motivating Dvořák and Mnich to conjecture that forbidding 4-cycles (i.e., requiring girth at least five) suffices to bring the fractional chromatic number strictly below 3. The conjecture remains open in general.
paper_contribution: Proves that the class of minimal obstructions of girth at least five to list $(3a:a)$-colorability is strongly hyperbolic (Theorem 5), which is stated as a key step in a forthcoming proof that the conjecture holds for planar graphs of girth at least five with bounded maximum degree.
paper_title: (3a:a)-list-colorability of embedded graphs of girth at least five
